cisCorePrinciples.name: Longevity and Independent Implementability
cisCorePrinciples.statement: CIS Core must be specified so that an implementer who has never met the original authors can build, verify, and evolve a conforming implementation years later without founder dependency.

cisCorePrinciples.name: Normative Stability
cisCorePrinciples.statement: Core terminology and constitutional requirements remain stable unless changed through the governed constitutional process.

cisCorePrinciples.name: Inheritance Over Redefinition
cisCorePrinciples.statement: Companion specifications, profiles, and documentation families inherit CIS terminology rather than redefining it.

cisCorePrinciples.name: Governance Continuity
cisCorePrinciples.statement: Governance is part of the specification surface and must be documented alongside the runtime.

conformanceCriteria.name: No Founder Dependency
conformanceCriteria.statement: A conforming implementation must not require private explanation, unpublished conventions, or hidden operator knowledge from the original authors.
conformanceCriteria.verification: An independent implementer can build, test, and evolve the system using only published CIS artifacts.

conformanceCriteria.name: Governance Process as Spec Surface
conformanceCriteria.statement: The governance process must be exposed as part of the specification surface.
conformanceCriteria.verification: The spec defines document status, stewardship, versioning, traceability, and release lifecycle.

conformanceCriteria.name: Model Agnosticism
conformanceCriteria.statement: The platform must remain agnostic to any specific reasoning engine or provider.
conformanceCriteria.verification: Routing and orchestration can target multiple compatible reasoning engines.

conformanceCriteria.name: Shared Artifact Governance
conformanceCriteria.statement: Major artifacts must follow the shared governance model with status, version, stewardship, classification, parent specification, traceability, and history fields.
conformanceCriteria.verification: Each major artifact is represented in the artifact governance registry with the required metadata.

coreSpecification.name: CIS Core
coreSpecification.inheritanceRule: Companion specifications inherit Core terminology and may not redefine constitutional terms.

companionSpecifications.name: Governed Knowledge Store Specification
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Canonical knowledge admission, replay, and trust lifecycle

companionSpecifications.name: Implementation Profile Freeze Criteria
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Objective gate for promoting profile artifacts from Draft to Frozen

companionSpecifications.name: Implementation Profile Worked-Example Policy
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Summary rule for the current frozen profile example set

companionSpecifications.name: Research OS
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Evidence, insights, ideas, and actions workflow

companionSpecifications.name: SOCK Alignment Specification
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Bridge CIS obligations to SOCK runtime semantics

companionSpecifications.name: CORI Alpha Proof Chain
companionSpecifications.responsibility: Minimum governed proof workflow for constitutional evidence

researchOS.name: Research OS
researchOS.role: Permanent institutional memory and improvement loop

traceabilityMatrix.requirement: CIS Core remains the single authoritative constitutional specification
Companion specifications inherit Core terminology rather than redefining it
Every artifact carries consistent governance status, stewardship, versioning, traceability, and release lifecycle fields
Implementation profiles adapt CIS for domain-specific constraints without changing constitutional requirements
Standards traceability answers requirement, component, evidence, replay, receipt, and test for each capability
Research OS preserves evidence, insights, ideas, and actions as durable institutional memory
Major artifacts follow the shared governance model
External standards families are mapped for interoperability and traceability
The conformance suite is generated from the traceability matrix source of truth
CORI Alpha is the first independently verifiable constitutional proof
traceabilityMatrix.test: tests/release/cori-alpha-proof.test.ts replay, receipt, and conformance coverage
